steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends with "the results of the meta-analysis suggest that ______" — we need a conclusion that follows logically from the stated evidence. The key facts: (1) mammal studies showed larger differences on average than bird studies, but (2) for every class of animals, there were individual studies showing differences well above the mammal average.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the studies...that examined mammals were more likely...to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental': The passage says nothing about whether effects were labeled detrimental. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the differences...are likely to be more pronounced for birds than for mammals': This reverses the stated comparison — mammals averaged larger differences. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the difference found in the study by Amy Morris-Drake...was likely larger than the average for studies of dwarf mongooses': The meta-analysis says every class had individual studies above the mammal average, but the passage gives no information about the Morris-Drake study's position relative to any average. ✗
  • Choice D — 'some studies of birds found larger effects...than some studies of mammals did': If bird studies reached differences well above the mammal average, and the mammal average sits above some mammal studies, then those bird studies exceeded some mammal studies. This follows directly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
